Title: IWS - Verify No Sensors Available state
Preconditions: 1. Q-SYS Designer design has no IWS Sensors added.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI.
Preconditions: 1. Observe the Web UI page content.

Title: 2. Verify the "Edit Zones" and "Multi-Sensor" tabs are visible but disabled/grayed out.
3. Verify the empty state message is displayed.
4. Attempt to click the disabled tabs.
Preconditions: 1. The page displays the message: "There are no IWS Sensors added to your design. Please add at least one to begin using this application."

Title: 2. A sensor icon is displayed above the message.
3. The "Edit Zones" and "Multi-Sensor" tabs are grayed out and non-interactive.
4. Cancel and Save buttons are present but no functional controls are available.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Default View with single sensor
Preconditions: 1. At least one IWS device is on the network and accessible.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI.
Preconditions: 1. Verify the "Single" tab is selected by default.

Title: 2. Verify the "Select a Sensor" dropdown is present and populated.
3. Observe the IR thermal frame with the People Count Zone polygon (yellow draggable handles).
4. Verify the sensor orientation widget (4 directional arrows) is displayed.
5. Verify the properties panel shows: Luminosity, Temperature, Humidity, Dew Point, Sound Level, TVOC, People Count, Occupancy.
6. Verify the "Edit" button is visible (blue, top-right).
7. Verify zoom controls (zoom in, zoom out, reset view) and fullscreen button are at the bottom.
Preconditions: 1. The Single tab is active with the sensor selected in the dropdown.

Title: 2. The IR thermal frame renders a live image with the People Count Zone polygon overlay and yellow draggable handles.
3. The orientation widget displays 4 directional arrows indicating sensor mounting direction.
4. The properties panel displays all sensor readings with appropriate units.
5. The People Count value is displayed at the top of the canvas area.
6. Zoom and fullscreen controls are functional at the bottom of the frame.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Edit Regions mode entry and settings panel
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network and accessible.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Default View.
Preconditions: 1. Click the "Edit" button (blue, top-right).

Title: 2. Verify the settings panel appears on the right with: Image Refresh Rate, Orientation Icon toggle, Color Scale, People Count Zone color.
3. Change Image Refresh Rate using the dropdown (default: 1 second).
4. Click the pause/play button next to the refresh rate.
5. Toggle the Orientation Icon on/off.
6. Verify the orientation icon appears/disappears on the IR frame.
Preconditions: 1. Clicking Edit enters Edit Regions mode.

Title: 2. The settings panel displays all controls: Image Refresh Rate (dropdown + pause/play), Orientation Icon (toggle), Color Scale (palette selector), People Count Zone (color swatch).
3. Image Refresh Rate can be changed via dropdown.
4. Pause button stops frame refresh; play resumes it.
5. Orientation Icon toggle shows/hides the directional indicator on the IR frame.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Color Scale selection in Edit Regions
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI.
3. Enter Edit Regions mode.
Preconditions: 1. Locate the Color Scale option in the settings panel.

Title: 2. Click the Color Scale selector.
3. Verify multiple palette options are available (rainbow, grayscale, hot, etc.).
4. Select a different palette.
5. Verify the IR frame updates to use the new color mapping.
6. Select each available palette and verify the frame updates.
Preconditions: 1. The Color Scale selector shows multiple palette options as gradient bars.

Title: 2. Selecting a palette immediately updates the IR frame rendering on the next refresh.
3. Thermal intensity gradients are clearly distinguishable in each palette.
4. No rendering errors occur when switching palettes.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify People Count Zone color picker
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
Preconditions: 1. Click the People Count Zone color swatch in the settings panel.

Title: 2. Verify a full color picker opens with gradient selector, hue bar, and hex input field.
3. Select a new color using the gradient picker.
4. Verify the hex value updates.
5. Enter a hex value manually (e.g., #FF0000).
6. Click outside the color picker to close it.
7. Verify the People Count Zone polygon on the IR frame reflects the new color.
Preconditions: 1. The color picker opens with a gradient area, hue/saturation bar, and hex input (#000000 format with opacity %).

Title: 2. Selecting a color updates the hex field in real-time.
3. Manual hex entry is accepted and the picker updates accordingly.
4. The People Count Zone polygon border color on the IR frame changes to match the selected color.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify People Count Zone polygon editing
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
Preconditions: 1. Observe the People Count Zone polygon overlaid on the IR frame (orange/yellow border with yellow handle dots).

Title: 2. Click and drag one of the yellow handle points.
3. Verify the polygon shape changes.
4. Drag multiple handles to reshape the zone.
5. Verify the zone boundary stays within the circular FOV boundary.
Preconditions: 1. The People Count Zone polygon is displayed with clearly visible yellow handle points at each vertex.

Title: 2. Handles are draggable and the polygon reshapes in real-time.
3. The polygon defines the area used for people counting.
4. The circular FOV boundary (orange border) indicates the physical sensor coverage area.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Presence Zone add and remove
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
3. People Count Zone is configured.
Preconditions: 1. In the left panel, locate "Presence Zones" section.

Title: 2. Click "+ Add Presence Zone".
3. Verify a new rectangular presence zone appears on the IR frame.
4. Verify "Presence Zone 1" appears in the zone list with X/Y coordinates.
5. Add additional presence zones (up to 5 total).
6. Verify each zone appears as a distinct colored rectangle on the frame.
7. Remove a presence zone by clicking its delete/remove control.
8. Verify the zone disappears from both the list and the IR frame.
Preconditions: 1. Clicking "+ Add Presence Zone" creates a new rectangular zone overlay on the IR frame.

Title: 2. Each presence zone is listed with its name and X/Y position values.
3. Up to 5 presence zones can be added (Presence Zone 1 through 5).
4. Each zone renders as a distinct colored rectangle on the thermal frame.
5. Zones can be removed, updating both the list and the visual overlay.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Presence Zone maximum limit (5 zones)
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
Preconditions: 1. Add presence zones one at a time until 5 zones exist.

Title: 2. Verify all 5 zones are listed (Presence Zone 1-5) with X/Y values.
3. Verify all 5 zones are visible as colored rectangles on the IR frame.
4. Attempt to add a 6th presence zone.
5. Verify the UI prevents adding more than 5 zones or the "+ Add Presence Zone" link is no longer available.
Preconditions: 1. Five presence zones can be created, each with a unique color and listed individually.

Title: 2. All 5 zones render simultaneously on the IR frame without overlapping labels.
3. The UI enforces a maximum of 5 presence zones.
4. The "+ Add Presence Zone" option is disabled or hidden when the limit is reached.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify No Presence Regions warning state
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
3. No presence zones are configured.
Preconditions: 1. Observe the Presence Zones section in the left panel.

Title: 2. Verify an informational message is displayed indicating no presence regions are configured.
3. Verify the "+ Add Presence Zone" link is visible and clickable.
4. Click "+ Add Presence Zone" to dismiss the warning state.
Preconditions: 1. When no presence zones exist, an info/warning message is displayed.

Title: 2. The "+ Add Presence Zone" link is prominently available.
3. Adding a presence zone transitions the UI out of the warning state to show the zone list.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Region Outside Zone validation
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
3. At least one presence zone is configured.
Preconditions: 1. Drag a presence zone rectangle partially or fully outside the People Count Zone polygon boundary.

Title: 2. Observe the visual feedback on the IR frame.
3. Verify the zone area outside the FOV boundary is indicated (red/pink region).
Preconditions: 1. The UI provides visual feedback when a presence zone is positioned outside the valid detection area.

Title: 2. The region outside the FOV boundary is clearly indicated with a distinct color (red/pink area).
3. The user is informed that zones outside the boundary may not produce valid detection data.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Reset Zones confirmation dialog
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
3. At least one zone (People Count or Presence) is configured.
Preconditions: 1. Locate and click the "Reset" button.

Title: 2. Verify a confirmation dialog appears with a warning message.
3. Verify the dialog has Cancel and Reset (destructive) action buttons.
4. Click Cancel and verify zones are unchanged.
5. Click Reset again and confirm the action.
6. Verify all zones are reset to default positions.
Preconditions: 1. Clicking Reset displays a confirmation dialog: "Reset Zones" with a warning about resetting zone configuration.

Title: 2. The dialog has a clearly labeled destructive Reset button.
3. Canceling the dialog preserves existing zone configuration.
4. Confirming the reset restores zones to their default positions/configuration.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Image Background Noise toggle
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
Preconditions: 1. Locate the "Image Background Noise" toggle in the settings panel.

Title: 2. Toggle the setting ON.
3. Observe the IR frame rendering.
4. Toggle the setting OFF.
5. Compare the frame rendering with the setting on vs. off.
Preconditions: 1. The Image Background Noise toggle is accessible in the settings panel.

Title: 2. Enabling the toggle applies background noise filtering to the thermal image.
3. Disabling the toggle shows the unfiltered thermal image.
4. The change is reflected on the next frame refresh.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Dark Mode toggle
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Edit Regions mode.
Preconditions: 1. Locate the "Dark Mode" toggle in the settings panel.

Title: 2. Toggle Dark Mode ON.
3. Observe the UI appearance changes.
4. Toggle Dark Mode OFF.
5. Verify the UI returns to the light theme.
Preconditions: 1. Enabling Dark Mode changes the UI theme/appearance.

Title: 2. Disabling Dark Mode restores the default light theme.
3. The toggle state is clearly indicated (on/off).
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Multi-Sensor view with sensor group
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are on the network and grouped.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI.
Preconditions: 1. Click the "Multiple" tab.

Title: 2. Verify the "Sensors" panel appears with "Select a Sensor Group" dropdown.
3. Select a sensor group (e.g., "IWS Sensor Group 1").
4. Verify all sensors in the group are listed (Sensor 01, 02, 03, 04) with individual people count values.
5. Verify the canvas shows all sensor IR frames arranged in a grid layout.
6. Verify the aggregate People Count is displayed at the top.
Preconditions: 1. The Multiple tab shows the multi-sensor view.

Title: 2. The sensor group dropdown lists available groups.
3. All sensors in the selected group are listed with their names and per-sensor people count.
4. The canvas displays all sensor IR frames in a default grid arrangement.
5. The total People Count at the top is the sum of all individual sensor counts.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify sensor arrangement drag and positioning
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are grouped.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Multiple tab.
3. Click Edit to enter edit mode.
Preconditions: 1. Verify Cancel and Save buttons appear (top-right).

Title: 2. Click and drag a sensor on the canvas to a new position.
3. Verify the sensor moves freely on the canvas.
4. Observe the properties bar at the bottom updates the Position X/Y values.
5. Arrange multiple sensors in a custom layout (not grid).
6. Click Save.
7. Reload the page and verify the arrangement persists.
Preconditions: 1. In edit mode, sensors can be dragged freely on the canvas.

Title: 2. The properties bar updates Position X and Y values in real-time as sensors are moved.
3. Sensors can be arranged in any custom spatial layout.
4. Clicking Save persists the arrangement.
5. The layout persists after page reload.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify sensor rotation, scale, and opacity controls
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are grouped.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Multiple tab, Edit mode.
Preconditions: 1. Select a sensor on the canvas or in the sensor list.

Title: 2. In the properties bar, change the Rotation value (degrees).
3. Verify the sensor rotates on the canvas.
4. Change the Scale value (%).
5. Verify the sensor scales on the canvas.
6. Change the Opacity value (%).
7. Verify the sensor opacity changes on the canvas.
8. Click Save and verify all changes persist.
Preconditions: 1. The properties bar shows Name, Group, Position (X/Y), Rotation (deg), Scale (%), and Opacity (%) for the selected sensor.

Title: 2. Rotation rotates the sensor view on the canvas by the specified degrees.
3. Scale increases/decreases the sensor view size proportionally.
4. Opacity changes the transparency of the sensor view on the canvas.
5. All property changes persist after Save.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Reset Multi Sensor Canvas
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are grouped.

Title: 2. Sensors have been arranged in a custom layout (non-default positions).
3. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Multiple tab.
Preconditions: 1. Click the "Reset" button at the bottom of the canvas.

Title: 2. Verify a confirmation dialog appears: "Reset Multi Sensor Canvas - Are you sure you want to reset the position, scale, and rotation of all the groups?"
3. Verify the message mentions fitting/resizing all sensors to the canvas.
4. Click Cancel and verify nothing changes.
5. Click Reset to confirm.
6. Verify all sensors return to default grid positions with default rotation, scale, and opacity.
Preconditions: 1. The Reset confirmation dialog warns about resetting position, scale, and rotation for all sensors in the group.

Title: 2. Canceling preserves the current arrangement.
3. Confirming the reset restores all sensors to their default grid layout with 100% scale, 0 rotation, and 100% opacity.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify multi-sensor layout persistence
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are grouped with a custom arrangement saved.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Multiple tab.
Preconditions: 1. Document the current sensor arrangement (positions, rotations, scales).

Title: 2. Close the browser and reopen the Web UI.
3. Navigate to the Multiple tab and select the same sensor group.
4. Verify the arrangement matches what was documented.
5. Power cycle one sensor in the group.
6. Reopen the Web UI and verify the layout.
Preconditions: 1. The multi-sensor layout persists after closing and reopening the Web UI.

Title: 2. The spatial arrangement is identical to what was saved.
3. Power cycling a single sensor does not affect the group layout.
4. All position, rotation, scale, and opacity values are preserved.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify sensor grouping in Multi-Sensor view
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the Multiple tab.

Title: 2. Use the "Select a Sensor Group" dropdown to view available groups.
3. Select a group and verify all member sensors are listed.
4. Verify each sensor shows its individual people count.
5. Verify the canvas renders all group members.
Preconditions: 1. The sensor group dropdown lists all configured groups.

Title: 2. Selecting a group populates the sensor list with all member devices.
3. Each sensor displays its individual people count (colored icon + number).
4. The canvas shows all group member sensor frames.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Disabled State for offline sensors
Preconditions: 1. Two or more IWS devices are grouped.

Title: 2. At least one sensor in the group is powered off or disconnected from the network.
3. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI in Multiple tab.
Preconditions: 1. Observe the warning banner at the top of the canvas area.

Title: 2. Verify the message reads: "Some of your sensors are offline. Connect them to see the thermal images from the device."
3. Identify the offline sensor(s) on the canvas.
4. Verify offline sensors render as gray placeholders with the orange FOV boundary but no thermal image.
5. Verify online sensors continue showing live thermal data.
6. Verify the People Count still aggregates from available (online) sensors.
7. Verify the sensor list still shows all sensors with their counts (offline sensors show 0).
Preconditions: 1. A warning banner is displayed indicating some sensors are offline.

Title: 2. Offline sensors appear as gray placeholder images with the FOV boundary outline visible.
3. Online sensors continue rendering live thermal data normally.
4. The total People Count reflects only data from online sensors.
5. The sensor list displays all sensors regardless of online status, with offline sensors showing 0 count.
6. Edit mode remains accessible for online sensors.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify popout card view
Preconditions: 1. IWS device(s) on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI.
Preconditions: 1. Trigger the popout card view (click the popout/expand control on a sensor).

Title: 2. Verify the card displays the sensor or group name.
3. Verify the card shows sensor thumbnail(s) and People Count.
4. Verify the card can be dismissed/closed.
Preconditions: 1. The popout card displays a compact view of the sensor/group with thumbnail images.

Title: 2. The People Count is shown on the card.
3. The card provides a quick overview without navigating away from the current view.
4. The card can be closed to return to the normal view.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify Single/Multiple tab switching
Preconditions: 1. At least one IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI.
Preconditions: 1. Verify the "Single" tab is selected by default.

Title: 2. Click the "Multiple" tab.
3. Verify the view switches to the multi-sensor canvas layout.
4. Click the "Single" tab.
5. Verify the view returns to the single-sensor view with dropdown, IR frame, and properties.
Preconditions: 1. Single tab shows the individual sensor view with Select a Sensor dropdown, IR frame, orientation widget, zone list, and properties panel.

Title: 2. Multiple tab shows the multi-sensor canvas with Select a Sensor Group dropdown, sensor list, and spatial arrangement.
3. Switching between tabs preserves the selected sensor/group state.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify zoom and fullscreen controls
Preconditions: 1. IWS device is on the network.

Title: 2. Open the IWS Sensor Web UI (Single or Multiple tab).
Preconditions: 1. Click the zoom in button (+magnifier icon).

Title: 2. Verify the canvas/IR frame zooms in.
3. Click the zoom out button (-magnifier icon).
4. Verify the canvas zooms out.
5. Click the reset view button.
6. Verify the canvas returns to default zoom level.
7. Click the fullscreen button.
8. Verify the canvas expands to fullscreen.
Preconditions: 1. Zoom in enlarges the canvas/frame view.

Title: 2. Zoom out reduces the canvas/frame view.
3. Reset view returns to the default zoom level and position.
4. Fullscreen expands the frame to fill the available viewport.
5. All zoom controls respond immediately without lag.
Preconditions: Manual
